data.title: Google (GOOGL) Stock Ties AI Growth to User Data as Gemini Gets Smarter
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) is moving its Gemini chatbot closer to a personal assistant that learns from each user over time. The company said Gemini will begin using data from Gmail, Search, YouTube, and Photos to improve its responses. As a result, responses may feel more relevant and less generic.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For example, Gemini could help plan a trip by reviewing past travel emails or saved photos. It may notice patterns like family travel or outdoor trips. Based on that context, it can suggest ideas without detailed prompts. Google says this shift aims to make Gemini more useful in everyday moments.

Meanwhile, GOOGL shares dropped slightly on Wednesday, closing at $335.84.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/15/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Tech Stocks Slip, Indexes Fall
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were trading mixed early Thursday as losses in Big Tech names dragged down major indexes. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.23% and 0.06%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.09%, at 3:08 a.m. EST on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed in the red, marking a second consecutive day of losses for them. The S&P 500 declined 0.53%, the Dow dropped 0.09%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.00%.

Yesterday, Chinese authorities told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds, leading to a slump in software stocks. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each dropped more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom (AVGO) slid 4% each. Moreover, NVDA (NVDA) shares dropped 1.4% on news that China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases.

Banks were also among the biggest laggards. Wells Fargo (WFC) dropped 4.6%, dragging the broader market lower after reporting weaker-than-expected Q4 revenue. Citigroup (C) and Bank of America (BAC) both fell more than 3%.

On the economic front, reports on initial weekly jobless claims and retail sales will be released today.

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), and BlackRock (BX).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.14%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.92 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,601 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as markets focused on the ongoing meeting between U.S., Greenland, and Danish officials regarding President Donald Trump's interest in acquiring the Danish territory of Greenland.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets fell on January 15 due to the ongoing U.S.-China chip trade tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.25%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 0.33%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.07%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 0.42%, but the Topix gained 0.68%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: OpenAI Bets $10 Billion on Cerebras Chips in Shift Away From Nvidia (NVDA)
data.description:
OpenAI has agreed to buy up to 750 megawatts of computing power from chipmaker Cerebras Systems over the next three years. The deal, valued at more than $10 billion, is among the largest infrastructure contracts in the AI industry to date. OpenAI plans to use Cerebras' technology to help run its AI products, including ChatGPT, and to make responses faster and more efficient.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The agreement is part of OpenAI's wider effort to expand its computing capacity as demand for AI tools continues to grow. Cerebras will build or lease new data centers that will host its chips, while OpenAI will pay to use the systems through 2028. Talks between the two companies began last August, after Cerebras showed that OpenAI's open-source models could run more efficiently on its hardware than on traditional graphics processing units.

Moving Beyond Nvidia

The partnership also signals a shift for OpenAI as it looks to diversify beyond Nvidia (NVDA), whose chips currently dominate the AI market. Cerebras makes large wafer-scale processors that can handle big AI tasks without needing thousands of smaller chips working together. This approach could help OpenAI reduce costs and lower delays when users interact with its AI tools. OpenAI said the goal is to make its models "respond much faster."

At the same time, the deal gives Cerebras a major customer ahead of its planned IPO. The company has been preparing for an initial public offering that could take place later this year or in early 2027. The new contract provides clear revenue visibility and helps Cerebras reduce its dependence on G42, a tech group based in the United Arab Emirates that has been one of its main clients.

The Broader Market Impact

The agreement reflects the rising importance of computing power as AI adoption accelerates across industries. Analysts say the 750-megawatt capacity that OpenAI will secure is comparable to several large data centers combined. This move could help OpenAI handle the growing costs tied to running complex AI systems. Chief Executive Sam Altman has said that OpenAI may invest up to $1.4 trillion to build 30 gigawatts of computing power in the years ahead.

Furthermore, the deal highlights new competition in the AI chip market. It could increase attention on Cerebras ahead of its listing, while also raising questions about how much Nvidia can continue to dominate the space. Other companies such as Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC) are also racing to expand their AI offerings. Meanwhile, Microsoft (MSFT), a key OpenAI partner, continues to integrate the startup's models into its Azure cloud platform.

Deals like this one suggest that computing power is turning into a core asset, much like cloud capacity was a decade ago.

By using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we've assembled notable big tech companies leading the AI race. This is a great tool to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader AI industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: "Major Mining Tie-Up" Rio Tinto and BHP Join Forces to Extract 200M Tonnes of Pilbara Iron Ore
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) and BHP (BHP) have agreed to extract up to 200 million tonnes of iron ore in Western Australia's Pilbara region by jointly developing neighboring deposits and sharing infrastructure. This marks one of the biggest collaborations between the two mining giants in years.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The companies signed two non‑binding memoranda of understanding (MOUs) to explore joint development opportunities across their neighboring Yandicoogina (Rio) and Yandi (BHP) sites.

The collaboration focuses on two key areas. These include developing Rio Tinto's Wunbye deposit, which remains an untapped resource with long-term potential, and supplying ore from BHP's Yandi Lower Channel Deposit for processing Rio Tinto's existing wet plants under agreed commercial terms.

Here's Why the Collaboration Matters 

Importantly, the Pilbara is one of the world's key iron ore regions, supplying huge volumes to steelmakers, especially in Asia. As ore grades decline and costs rise, executives from both miners noted that the initiative aims to boost productivity without heavy new capital spending.

Rio Tinto's iron ore chief, Matthew Holcz, said both companies can "better leverage existing infrastructure to unlock additional production with minimal capital requirements." At the same time, BHP's WA Iron Ore president Tim Day called it "a clear example of productivity in action," highlighting the value created by sharing expertise and infrastructure.

Moreover, both companies said the partnership builds on a 2023 agreement that allowed them to start mining ore along a shared boundary that was previously off-limits.

Who Is Better, BHP or Rio?

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Alibaba Deepens Consumer AI Push With New Everyday Qwen Features
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) is pushing its consumer AI strategy further, expanding the Qwen app from a chatbot into a tool that can handle everyday tasks. On Thursday, Alibaba said it has upgraded Qwen so users can order food delivery, book travel, and complete payments directly within the chat. The new features are now in public testing in China and allow users to finish tasks without switching between different apps.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Qwen Becomes a One-Stop App for Daily Use

The update builds on changes Alibaba rolled out in mid-November, when Qwen first entered public beta as part of a broader move into consumer-facing AI. Until then, Alibaba had focused mainly on enterprise AI through its cloud business, while rivals such as ByteDance and Tencent (TCEHY) moved faster in consumer applications.

With the latest upgrade, Qwen can now carry out tasks linked to real services, instead of just answering questions. The app brings several Alibaba platforms into one place, including shopping site Taobao, payment app Alipay, travel service Fliggy, and mapping app Amap.

With Alipay built in, users can approve and complete payments without leaving the conversation. Alibaba said the feature currently supports instant commerce orders and will expand to more services over time.

Qwen Draws Early Users as AI Competition Builds

Alibaba said Qwen has crossed 100 million monthly active users within two months of its mid-November public beta launch. The app runs on Alibaba's Qwen3 foundation model.

The push comes as AI agents gain traction worldwide, with companies like Meta Platforms (META) and OpenAI also rolling out tools that handle bookings and multi-step tasks.

If Qwen becomes part of daily habits like shopping and travel, it could deepen user engagement across Alibaba's ecosystem and support its longer-term AI growth plans.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now?

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 13 Buy ratings and one Hold over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.66, suggesting roughly 19.87% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Edge Higher after S&P 500 (SPX) Extends Its Losing Streak
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher ahead of Thursday's session after the S&P 500 (SPX) came off a second straight day of losses. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index were up a modest 0.04%, 0.06%, and 0.08%, respectively, at 1:00 a.m. EDT on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On Wednesday evening, President Donald Trump signed a proclamation placing a 25% tariff on certain semiconductor imports. The measure does not apply to chips brought in to support the expansion of the U.S. technology supply chain.

By the close of trading on Wednesday, the S&P 500 fell 0.5%, the Dow slipped about 42 points, or nearly 0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 1%. All three major indexes closed lower for a second straight day.

Stocks finished lower as losses in large technology stocks dragged on the major indexes. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each fell more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom (AVGO) slid about 4%, while Nvidia (NVDA) dipped 1.4%.

Looking ahead to Thursday, investors will turn their attention to earnings from Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), and BlackRock (BLK). Weekly jobless claims data is also scheduled for release.

data.title: M&A News: Calavo Growers Stock (CVGW) Soars on $430M Acquisition by Mission Produce
data.description:
Calavo Growers (CVGW) stock rallied 13% in Wednesday's after-hours trading on news of the company's acquisition by avocado producer Mission Produce (AVO) in a deal valued at $430 million. Calavo Growers is a provider of fresh avocados, tomatoes, papayas, and value-added prepared foods such as guacamole. The acquisition is expected to be completed by August 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here Is Why Mission Produce Is Acquiring Calavo Growers

Under the deal, Mission Produce will pay Calavo shareholders $27 per share, including $14.85 in cash and 0.9790 shares of AVO for each share of CVGW. Following the completion of the deal, Mission Produce shareholders will own about 80.3% of the combined company, while Calavo shareholders will own the remaining 19.7%. Overall, the deal is valued at a total enterprise value of about $430 million. The transaction price reflects a 26% premium to Calavo's 30-trading day volume weighted average price of $21.41.

Mission Produce expects its vertically integrated platform and global distribution network to be enhanced by Calavo's complementary sourcing, prepared foods capabilities, and solid customer base. Furthermore, the deal is expected to bolster Mission Produce's position in the premium avocado market in North America and help in establishing a fresh produce platform to capture the rising demand for healthy and convenient foods.

Mission Produce expects the Calavo acquisition to generate $25 million in annualized cost synergies within 18 months following the completion of the deal. The company expects the deal to significantly boost its EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) growth and cash flow generation.

Is AVO Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, Mission Produce scores a Moderate Buy rating. Roth MKM analyst Gerry Sweeney has a Buy rating on Mission Produce stock with a price target of $17, indicating a 34% upside potential.

Meanwhile, Calavo Growers has not been assigned any ratings over the past three months.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: TSM Earnings: TSMC Smashes Q4 Estimates as AI Chip Demand Soars
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., or TSMC, (TSM) reported a 35% jump in fourth-quarter profit, surpassing estimates and hitting a new record, driven by robust demand for AI chips. Meanwhile, revenue came in at NT$1.046 trillion ($33.73 billion), beating the expected NT$1.034 trillion. The strong results marked eight consecutive quarters of year-over-year profit growth for the world's largest contract chipmaker.

data.title: China Is Setting Rules to Limit Nvidia's H200 Chip Purchases
data.description:
Beijing is reportedly drafting rules to cap imports of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) chips from Nvidia (NVDA) and other foreign players, according to Nikkei Asia. Regulators are working on defining how many chips a company can buy, alongside strict requirements of domestic chip purchases.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Through these regulations, Beijing is striving to balance its push for domestic chip innovation with the needs of Chinese tech giants for access to top-tier hardware. Yesterday, NVDA shares dropped 1.4% on news that China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases.

Beijing to Regulate Import Volumes

The regulations would cap total volumes of cutting-edge AI chips local firms can buy, allowing limited Nvidia sales instead of an outright ban. Beijing could grant its first approvals for limited H200 chip purchases by month's end, though companies must justify their needs. Officials will likely decide which firms receive how many chips, though details remain unfinalized.

The Chinese government is reportedly holding regular meetings with top tech firms to understand their use of foreign chips, asking for explanations on why domestic alternatives cannot replace them, particularly for less intensive tasks like inferencing, or deploying trained AI models. Officials have also begun soliciting Chinese tech firms' views on demand for Nvidia's next-gen Blackwell chips, which Washington has yet to approve for export.

Despite Washington's recent reversal allowing H200 sales, Beijing has withheld import approvals to protect local chipmakers like Huawei, Horizon Robotics, Cambricon, and emerging players such as Moore Threads, MetaX, Biren Technology, and Suiyuan Technology.

Nvidia Caught in the Crosshairs

The U.S. government recently approved the sale of Nvidia's H200 GPUs (graphics processing units) to China, with conditions such as revenue-sharing and limited share of shipments relative to domestic sales.

Chinese tech giants like Alibaba (BABA), Tencent (TCEHY), and ByteDance continue to prioritize Nvidia's AI server racks for their unmatched computing power, which fuels ambitions in training, inference, and simulations. Against this backdrop, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang noted this month that while the H200 remains competitive in China for now, "it won't be competitive forever." He added that the company will eventually have to roll out its Blackwell and Rubin chips in China on time, subject to approval. Washington's abrupt policy shift marks a stark reversal from its prior efforts to bar China from advanced technologies.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 40 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $264.30 implies 44.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have gained 34.4%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Rocket Lab (RKLB) Stock Surged 6% to a Record High
data.description:
Shares of space launch company Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) jumped more than 6% on Wednesday, as satellite-related stocks extended their rally amid rising geopolitical tensions. In addition, Cantor Fitzgerald five-star analyst Andres Sheppard reiterated his Buy rating on RKLB, calling Rocket Lab the top alternative to Elon Musk's SpaceX. RKLB shares reached a new high of $91.80, but fell 2% in after-hours trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Overall, the satellite sector is picking up steam as global tensions rise, driven by recent White House moves and sharp rhetoric around Venezuela, Iran, and even Denmark, tied to President Trump's renewed focus on Greenland. Other players in the sector, including Planet Labs (PL) and EchoStar (SATS), also climbed roughly 6% on Wednesday.

Cantor Fitzgerald Stays Bullish on RKLB

Sheppard's positive outlook was driven by Rocket Lab's strong execution, highlighted by a record third quarter and a major contract win from the U.S. Space Development Agency.

Additionally, Rocket Lab's recent contract from the U.S. Space Development Agency for $816 million is the largest in the company's history. The award effectively doubled Rocket Lab's total backlog, marking a major step forward for the business. Under the contract, Rocket Lab will design and manufacture 18 missile-warning, tracking, and defense satellites for low Earth orbit.

Sheppard also pointed to Rocket Lab's first Neutron launch, expected in the first half of 2026, as the single most important catalyst for the stock.

On the downside, he sees some risks for Rocket Lab, including possible Neutron delays, regulatory challenges, and supply-chain disruptions. Overall, he has strong confidence in Rocket Lab's proven launch track record and its evolution into a full-scale space infrastructure company.

What Should Investors Watch Next?

Rocket Lab completed 21 Electron launches in fiscal 2025, its highest annual total to date, bringing its successful mission count to 79. That track record makes the company the third-most active launch provider globally, second in the U.S. behind SpaceX.

Looking ahead, both investors and analysts are focused on Neutron, Rocket Lab's medium-lift, reusable launch vehicle.

Meanwhile, management expects Neutron to reach the launchpad in Q1 2026, with its first flight soon after. If successful, Neutron could significantly improve Rocket Lab's unit economics and position the company as the only credible commercial alternative to SpaceX's Falcon 9.

Is RKLB a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, RKLB stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with six Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Rocket Lab is $71.43, suggesting a potential downside of 22.19% from the current level.

RKLB shares have surged more than 250% in the last 12 months.
data.author: Kirti Tak
